page: Amazon Web Services c8id.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 96 physical cores and 192 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a compute-focused ratio of 2.0 GB of RAM per physical core. The processor architecture includes an extensive L3 cache of 480 MB and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations.

For local storage and data-intensive operations, the server includes 11,400 GB of direct-attached NVMe SSD instance storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 75 Gbps. In benchmark evaluations, the c8id.metal-48xl demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core tests and Passmark CPU scores. Its memory subsystem also achieves top-tier status, delivering uncached read bandwidth of 599.05 GB/sec and write bandwidth of 252.62 GB/sec.

The hardware configuration delivers high resource density and cost efficiency for workloads that can exploit its massive parallel processing capabilities and local NVMe storage, though the 2.0 GB RAM-per-core ratio represents a tradeoff for memory-bound applications. Benchmark results indicate exceptional suitability for high-throughput database operations, Redis deployments, and CPU-based LLM inference, where it achieves top-tier prompt processing speeds. It is highly suited for multi-threaded compilation, high-performance computing (HPC), and large-scale web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services c8id.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 192 vCPUs across 96 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors running at 3.9 GHz. It provides 384.0 GB of system memory and 11,400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, supported by a 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The architecture integrates a 480 MB L3 cache and supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instructions. In benchmark testing, the server delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and high-throughput database execution. This hardware profile is highly cost-efficient for parallel computing and localized data processing, though the 2.0 GB RAM-per-core ratio presents a tradeoff for memory-intensive workloads. It is optimized for high-performance computing, multi-threaded software compilation, database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server with 192 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 11,400 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core CPU and database performance.

tagline: A bare-metal, compute-optimized platform featuring 192 vCPUs, massive local NVMe storage, and advanced matrix acceleration for high-throughput parallel workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors with 192 vCPUs and 3.9 GHz clock speed
11,400 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated matrix math
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer
Best suited for database operations, multi-threaded compilation, and CPU-based inference
